Title: The Martian
Author: Andy Weir
Notes: Genuinely could not put it down. The problem-solving is what got me. Mateo borrowed it after.

Title: Project Hail Mary
Author: Andy Weir
Notes: Even better than The Martian. Rocky. Enough said.

Title: The Long Way to a Small Angry Planet
Author: Becky Chambers
Notes: Slower paced but really cozy for a sci-fi book. The crew dynamics are the whole thing.

Title: A Fire Upon the Deep
Author: Vernor Vinge
Notes: Ambitious. Lost me in the middle section. The Tines are a great idea though.

Title: Recursion
Author: Blake Crouch
Notes: Hard to put down. The memory timeline stuff broke my brain in a good way.

Title: Dark Matter
Author: Blake Crouch
Notes: Basically the same premise as Recursion but still gripping. Read it in two weeks.

Title: Flowers for Algernon
Author: Daniel Keyes
Notes: Assigned myself this one. Hit harder than expected. Charlie Gordon broke me a little.

Title: Ender's Game
Author: Orson Scott Card
Notes: Knew the twist going in and it still landed. Very readable.

Title: All Systems Red (Murderbot Diaries 1)
Author: Martha Wells
Notes: Short but perfect. Murderbot's voice is exactly my kind of humor. Starting the series.

Title: Artificial Condition (Murderbot Diaries 2)
Author: Martha Wells
Notes: Just as good. ART is incredible.

Title: Rogue Protocol (Murderbot Diaries 3)
Author: Martha Wells
Notes: Still good. Slightly weaker than the first two but still fun.

Title: Exit Strategy (Murderbot Diaries 4)
Author: Martha Wells
Notes: Great ending to the first arc. Cried a little. Not sorry.

Title: Klara and the Sun
Author: Kazuo Ishiguro
Notes: Beautifully sad. Klara's perspective is unlike anything I have read.

Title: The Dispossessed
Author: Ursula K. Le Guin
Notes: Dense but worth it. The dual-timeline structure is smart. Le Guin is something else.

Title: Leviathan Wakes (Expanse 1)
Author: James S.A. Corey
Notes: Great. The scope is huge but the characters keep it grounded. Starting Book 2.

Title: Caliban's War (Expanse 2)
Author: James S.A. Corey
Notes: Good. Avasarala is my favorite character in the series so far.
